name: Barber, salon, and grooming
pitchTone: Friendly, low-key, and respectful of chair-side time. Speak like a regular customer who has noticed their work, and assume the owner is in the middle of a cut or a client.
valuePropHook: A flat $450 single-page site that shows your best cuts, your booking link, hours, and how to find the chair, so new clients walking by Instagram or Yelp can decide in ten seconds whether to come in.

objections.objection: My existing booking app already does that.
objections.response: Totally, and we link straight to it. The page sits in front of your booking app so people who find you on Google or Yelp see the work, the hours, and a one-tap link instead of bouncing off a thin profile.

objections.objection: I just use Instagram for everything.
objections.response: Makes sense, and we point right at it. The page is the home base that shows the cuts, the hours, the address, and the booking link, so DMs do not have to repeat the same answers every day.
Good problem to have. This is more about the new client who searches before they walk in: it gives them the hours, the chair, the price tier, and a tap-to-call button so they actually show up.

objections.objection: I do not have good photos yet.
objections.response: No worries. We start with what you have on Instagram or your phone and leave clean placeholders; you can swap photos in later without paying again.

objections.objection: I cannot afford a website right now.
objections.response: Fair. The whole point is that it is a flat $450 one-time fee, hosted, with no monthly. If a single extra cut a month covers it, the rest is upside.

objections.objection: I do not want clients booking online with strangers.
objections.response: Understood. The page does not have to take bookings; it can simply send people to call, text, or DM you, so you still gatekeep who sits in the chair.

objectionMap.skeptical: Fair question. I am Callan from callmemaybe. I was checking public barber and salon listings for whether new clients can quickly see cuts, hours, location, and how to book.
objectionMap.ai_disclosure: Yes, I am an AI voice operator for callmemaybe, and this call is automated and recorded. I can keep it quick or remove you if you do not want the call.
objectionMap.number_source: I used public business listing research for the shop, not a private contact list. If you want no more calls, say stop and I will mark it.
objectionMap.pricing: It is a flat $450 for a photo-led page with hours, location, booking or DM link, and proof from your work. Nothing starts unless you choose to pay the invoice.
objectionMap.send_info: I can send the short version through AgentMail. What email should I use? I will read it back before anything goes out.
objectionMap.call_later: No problem. What exact time works around clients? I do not want to interrupt you mid-cut again.

reviewValueProps: Turn review mentions of clean fades, friendly service, and loyal clients into proof beside the booking CTA.
Make hours, chair location, price feel, and booking or DM link obvious for first-time clients.
Use existing Instagram or review photos as the first visual proof instead of a generic salon page.
siteTemplateHint: Use a warm, photo-led barber template: dark, masculine palette with one bold accent, big hero image of the chair or a finished cut, services with simple price tier copy, hours, and a sticky tap-to-call button.
customerPersonaHint: Local clients searching for a new barber or stylist, scrolling fast on a phone, looking for photos of work, a price feel, hours, and a quick way to call or DM.

marketSignals.label: Booking link, hours, or chair location is hard to find.
marketSignals.evidenceHint: Listing, Instagram, or reviews mention appointments but no owned page connects them.

marketSignals.label: Public profiles show work but do not convert searchers into bookings.
marketSignals.evidenceHint: Instagram or review photos exist without clear hours, prices, or booking path.

marketSignals.label: New clients cannot tell whether walk-ins, appointments, or DMs are preferred.
marketSignals.evidenceHint: Listing lacks booking instructions or review questions mention scheduling.

leadSources: Google Maps barber and salon categories
Instagram profile links from public listings

serviceOffer.headline: Photo-led booking page for a barber or salon
serviceOffer.customerOutcome: New clients can see the work, hours, location, and booking or DM path without digging.

packages.name: Chair page
packages.description: Photo-led page with service tiers, hours, location, booking link, and hosting.

packages.name: Photo refresh
packages.description: Swap in new cuts, styles, or seasonal service copy.

serviceOffer.refundPolicy: Refund before build work starts if the owner cannot approve usable photos or booking details.

vendorRequirements: designer review for weak photo sets
operator review before using client images from public profiles
qaRules: hours and address are visible on mobile
service tier copy avoids unverified price promises

portalCopy.intakePrompt: Confirm booking link, hours, services, photo permissions, and preferred client contact path.

reviewStrategy: request reviews about service, style, and repeat-client trust
collect best style mentions for future proof copy

marginModel.notes: Lower base price is offset by repeat photo-refresh opportunities.
